description:
You are assisting a capacity-planning engineer who has gathered daily resource-usage samples for several application servers.  
    The samples are stored as individual INI-formatted files inside the directory  
    /home/user/capacity_planner/data/  

    Example filename pattern (already present on disk):  
        serverXX.ini   – where XX is a two-digit identifier such as 01, 02, 03 …

    INI file structure (identical for every file):  
        • Each INI has exactly three sections, one per day, matching the ISO-8601 date of the measurement (YYYY-MM-DD).  
        • Inside every section there are precisely three keys:
              CPU       – integer, average CPU load percentage observed that day  
              Memory    – integer, average RAM consumption in MiB  
              Disk      – integer, average disk-space consumption in GiB  
        • No extra keys or sections exist.

    Your tasks:

    1. Parse every *.ini file inside /home/user/capacity_planner/data/  
       For each file, calculate the arithmetic mean for CPU, Memory and Disk across all sections it contains.

    2. Decide whether the server is in a “capacity-risk” state.  
       A server is considered at risk when at least one of the following conditions is true for its calculated 3-day average:  
           • Avg CPU  ≥ 50 %  
           • Avg Memory ≥ 8000 MiB  
           • Avg Disk ≥ 200 GiB

    3. For every server that meets the risk criteria, write one line to the log file  
       /home/user/capacity_planner/summary/capacity_risk_2024-08.log  
       The file MUST be created by your script; the directory /home/user/capacity_planner/summary/ may or may not already exist.  

       Strict log-file requirements (checked by automated tests):  
          • The file must be plain text, LF-terminated.  
          • The very first line is an exact header:  
                SERVER_ID|AVG_CPU|AVG_MEMORY|AVG_DISK  
          • One subsequent line per at-risk server, ordered by ascending server ID (numerical order).  
          • Per-line format:  
                <server_id>|<avg_cpu>|<avg_memory>|<avg_disk>  
              − server_id  : the basename of the INI file without extension (e.g. server01)  
              − All numeric averages rounded to exactly two decimal places (0.00).  
          • No extra spaces anywhere.  
          • Example of a fully compliant two-server file (do NOT hard-code):  
                SERVER_ID|AVG_CPU|AVG_MEMORY|AVG_DISK  
                server01|57.67|6923.33|118.33  
                server02|72.67|11916.67|249.00

    4. Create a completion marker file called  
       /home/user/capacity_planner/summary/analysis.done  
       The file must contain the single word  
            OK  
       followed by a trailing newline.  This marker allows the automation to detect that all steps finished.

    5. Any intermediate scratch files should be placed only under  
       /home/user/capacity_planner/tmp/  
       (Create that sub-directory if you need it).  They will not be evaluated.

    Hints & constraints (not enforced by tests, but useful):  
       • Tools available include awk, grep, cut, sed, jq, python, etc.  
       • You may find it easiest to read INI data with a short Python or awk script.  
       • Parallelising the per-file average calculation is encouraged; using GNU parallel or xargs ‑P is acceptable.  
       • Make sure the summary directory exists before writing the two report files.  
       • Double-check numeric rounding and ordering; those are the most common sources of test failure.

    When you are finished, both of the following must exist and exactly match the specification:

        1) /home/user/capacity_planner/summary/capacity_risk_2024-08.log
        2) /home/user/capacity_planner/summary/analysis.done
truth:
<!--
        Ground-truth data for the automated checker.
        These files are pre-populated before the agent starts.
    -->

    <!-- Source INI files available to the agent -->
    <initialFiles>
        <file path="/home/user/capacity_planner/data/server01.ini"><![CDATA[
[2024-08-01]
CPU=55
Memory=6820
Disk=120

[2024-08-02]
CPU=60
Memory=7000
Disk=118

[2024-08-03]
CPU=58
Memory=6950
Disk=117
]]></file>

        <file path="/home/user/capacity_planner/data/server02.ini"><![CDATA[
[2024-08-01]
CPU=70
Memory=12000
Disk=250

[2024-08-02]
CPU=75
Memory=11850
Disk=249

[2024-08-03]
CPU=73
Memory=11900
Disk=248
]]></file>

        <file path="/home/user/capacity_planner/data/server03.ini"><![CDATA[
[2024-08-01]
CPU=30
Memory=4000
Disk=80

[2024-08-02]
CPU=28
Memory=3950
Disk=79

[2024-08-03]
CPU=27
Memory=3900
Disk=78
]]></file>
    </initialFiles>

    <!-- Expected artefacts AFTER the agent completes -->
    <expectedFiles>

        <file path="/home/user/capacity_planner/summary/capacity_risk_2024-08.log"><![CDATA[
SERVER_ID|AVG_CPU|AVG_MEMORY|AVG_DISK
server01|57.67|6923.33|118.33
server02|72.67|11916.67|249.00
]]></file>

        <file path="/home/user/capacity_planner/summary/analysis.done"><![CDATA[OK
]]></file>

    </expectedFiles>
